meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve overwhelmed by AI tools (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome overwhelmed by AI tools. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: overwhelmed by AI tools

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Advanced Checklist for Freelance Developers
content.seo_description: Master Git with AI to eliminate development overwhelm. A high-level checklist for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting fragmented AI outputs. By integrating Git with AI-native tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you create a deterministic, version-controlled feedback loop. This architecture allows you to ship complex features rapidly, maintain codebase integrity, and deliver production-ready software while keeping your workflow clean and client-ready.
content.intro:
The modern freelance developer is buried under a landslide of AI-generated code snippets, abandoned branches, and context drift. You start a feature with high hopes, but five AI interactions later, your codebase is a chaotic labyrinth of hallucinated dependencies and broken logic. The speed of AI has outpaced your ability to manage it.

Relief lies in rigorous version control. By forcing your AI agents to operate within a strict Git-native framework, you transform them from chaotic code-generators into disciplined software engineers. It’s time to stop treating AI as an oracle and start treating it as a developer under your strict, version-controlled command.

steps.title: Establish the AI-Safe Baseline
steps.description: Before any AI intervention, create a clean state. This prevents context poisoning by ensuring the agent only sees optimized, relevant code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and identify the specific module boundaries. Propose a clean, modular structure that minimizes coupling before we start feature development.

steps.title: Contextual Semantic Branching
steps.description: Use Git branches to isolate AI experiments. This allows you to discard failed AI logic without dirtying your main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a summary of the current task requirements and translate them into a set of Git-compliant atomic commit messages for the upcoming work.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Refactor authentication module to support OAuth2' --branch-name feature/auth-refactor

steps.title: Automated Code Review Loops
steps.description: Use Claude Code to review its own changes before committing. This enforces a 'Self-Correction' workflow that drastically reduces bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a rigorous peer review on the last three commits. Identify potential edge cases, security vulnerabilities, or performance bottlenecks.

steps.title: Deterministic Integration Testing
steps.description: Ensure AI-generated features pass your suite. Never commit without a passing test state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the changes made, write comprehensive unit tests in Vitest that cover the new functionality and edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run-tests && git add . && git commit -m 'feat: implement auth with passing tests'

steps.title: Final Sync and Documentation
steps.description: Sync your AI-driven documentation with your code commits to ensure the client understands the architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Update the README.md and generate a technical changelog based on the recent commit history.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --doc --output README.md && git push origin feature/auth-refactor

pro_tips: Always use a 'git reset --hard' if the AI starts hallucinating; don't try to fix AI errors manually.
Tag your commits with [AI] prefixes to track which parts of your history were machine-generated for easier auditing.
Limit the context window by ignoring your 'dist' or 'node_modules' folders in your .claudeignore file.
Use git bisect to pinpoint exactly which AI-generated commit introduced a regression.
Treat the Claude Code CLI as a junior dev; provide specific instructions, then verify every output before staging.
resources: Git Best Practices for AI: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Integrate a CI/CD pipeline like GitHub Actions to run these Claude Code review steps automatically on every pull request.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn AI chaos into a scalable freelance business. Don't let the tools overwhelm you; master the architecture and reclaim your time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Claude Code to review its own changes before committing. This enforces a 'Self-Correction' workflow that drastically reduces bugs.
